tax: Placed at family rank, Rime Glacials is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a creeping rime-ice body. A new genus belongs here only if it shows a creeping ice body with its own cold seep.
ap: The grouping is recognised by an advancing rime-front. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — breath-frost and a rime-sheet — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Rime Glacials occupies cold seeps and ice-rimed galleries. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where water-pressure freezes a cold seep and recur with the seep refreezes, sustaining themselves on cold-seep water.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: creeps as frost, freezes feet to lock prey. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: rime body and cold-sense. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each genus and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: freezing-advance and immobilising. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Rime Glacials matters because locks crews in cold galleries. It lets survey reports sort creeping-frost hazards from flung-ice ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Rime Glacials is the family-rank grouping of creeping ground-ice elementals of the rime kind within the True Elementals. Its members share a creeping rime body that freezes the ground and locks prey, and divide below into genus groups by seep chemistry and creep rate. The immobilising frost of the cold galleries.
